methods.method_name: Air Sample Collection and Pollen Analysis
methods.description: Process for collecting air samples using a Burkard spore and pollen trap and analyzing them microscopically to study pollen distribution patterns.

steps.description: Air samples were collected with a Burkard spore and pollen trap (Hirst type) in all cities.
steps.searchable_summary: collect air samples using Burkard spore and pollen trap

steps.description: Sampling of airborne pollen was conducted daily from the 1st of March until the 31st of October in all the sampling sites and throughout all the years under observation.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct daily pollen sampling from March to October

steps.description: The samples collected were delivered to Vinnytsia for microscopic analysis and were analyzed at 400× magnification with an optical microscope (LM) by the method of three horizontal transects (years 2009-2011) or 12 vertical transects (years 2012-2016).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen samples using optical microscope at 400x magnification

methods.description: Statistical analysis of seasonal patterns of ragweed pollen distribution using tools from the European Aeroallergen Network.

steps.description: The analysis of seasonal patterns of ragweed pollen distribution - in particular, the onset and end dates of the pollen release season as well as peak concentrations - was conducted using the statistical tools of European Aeroallergen Network (based in Vienna, Austria).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze seasonal pollen distribution using EAN tools

methods.description: Development and use of a complex model for ragweed pollen dispersal using SILAM, considering various environmental and biological factors.

steps.description: The complex model of ragweed pollen dispersal was designed on the basis of the System for Integrated modeLling of Atmospheric coMposition (SILAM) in collaboration with the Finnish Meteorological Institute (Helsinki) where this system was developed.
steps.searchable_summary: design ragweed pollen dispersal model using SILAM

steps.description: The development of the model for Ambrosia is based on a double-threshold temperature sum model that describes the dynamics of the flowering season and is analogous to the thermal time models employed to predict the onset and duration of flowering.
steps.searchable_summary: develop Ambrosia model using double-threshold temperature sum

steps.description: Another parameter considered whilst modeling the Ambrosia pollen release season is the duration of daylight; ragweed is a short-day plant, thus, it begins flowering when the day length shortens and the photoperiod is about 14.5 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: incorporate daylight duration into Ambrosia model

steps.description: The model was used to simulate the invasion of A. artemisiifolia in Europe. The simulation takes into account the climate suitability (temperature during the vegetative season, annual variations in dormancy-breaking temperature, available moisture, etc.), transport and dispersal of seeds from infested areas, suitability of land use for ragweed propagation as well as quarantine measures aimed at controlling the spread of the plant.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate A. artemisiifolia invasion in Europe using model

steps.description: The accuracy was calibrated against the actual pollen concentration data obtained in Ukraine.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate model accuracy using Ukraine pollen data

methods.description: Visualization of the modeling results using pollen mapping tools and comparison with aerobiological data.

steps.description: Results of modeling were visualized using the pollen mapping tools of the Grid Analysis and Display System.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize modeling results using Grid Analysis and Display System

steps.description: Simulation results were compared against data of aerobiological investigations available for six monitoring stations in Ukraine.
steps.searchable_summary: compare simulation results with aerobiological data

steps.description: To develop the maps of Ukraine, a scale of 1:10,000,000 was adopted as being optimal for general mapping of the country territory.
steps.searchable_summary: develop maps of Ukraine at 1:10,000,000 scale
